M1.name: Strategy and Planning
M1.description: Covers the overarching information security governance framework: leadership commitment, ISMS policy, roles and responsibilities, organisational structure, confidentiality agreements, and communication with external parties and authorities.
M1.not_includes: Risk assessment and treatment (M2), awareness and training programmes (M3), HR employment lifecycle controls (M4), detailed legal and regulatory compliance (M5), and ISMS performance measurement and internal audit (M6).

M2.name: Information Security Risk Management
M2.description: Covers the full information security risk lifecycle: identification, analysis, evaluation, treatment selection, risk treatment plans, statement of applicability, and ongoing review and communication of risk.
M2.not_includes: Day-to-day operational controls (T1–T9), security awareness activities (M3), and generic ISMS governance statements (M1).

M3.name: Awareness and Training
M3.description: Covers security awareness and training: identifying training needs, planning and delivering awareness programmes, measuring effectiveness, and maintaining training records for all relevant personnel.
M3.not_includes: HR screening and employment conditions (M4), technical access control configuration (T5), and performance measurement of the ISMS (M6).

M4.name: Human Resources Security
M4.description: Covers security aspects of the HR lifecycle: pre-employment screening, contractual security clauses, responsibilities during employment, disciplinary process, termination and change of employment, return of assets, and revocation of access.
M4.not_includes: Security awareness programme content (M3), technical access provisioning details (T5), and generic organisational governance (M1).

M5.name: Compliance
M5.description: Covers legal, regulatory, statutory, and contractual compliance related to information security, including privacy and data protection obligations, intellectual property rights, and compliance checking against internal policies and external standards.
M5.not_includes: Operational monitoring and logging (T3), risk management (M2), and generic governance statements (M1).

M6.name: Performance Evaluation and Improvement
M6.description: Covers monitoring and measuring the performance of the ISMS, internal audits, management reviews, corrective and preventive actions, and continual improvement processes.
M6.not_includes: Incident handling (T8), day-to-day operations (T3), and primary risk assessment activities (M2).

T1.name: Asset Management
T1.description: Covers information and asset management: asset inventory, ownership, classification, labelling, acceptable use, handling of removable media, and secure disposal of assets and information.
T1.not_includes: Physical protection of premises and equipment (T2), and third-party service provider controls (T6).

T2.name: Physical and Environmental Security
T2.description: Covers physical and environmental protection of information processing facilities: secure areas, entry controls, equipment security, environmental protections, clear desk and clear screen, and secure removal or reuse of equipment.
policy_document_types: Clear Desk and Clear Screen Policy
T2.not_includes: Logical access controls (T5), asset classification and ownership (T1), and incident response (T8).

T3.name: Operations Management
T3.description: Covers IT operational procedures and responsibilities: documented operating procedures, change management, separation of environments, capacity management, backup, malware protection, logging, log monitoring, and clock synchronisation.
T3.not_includes: Network design and communication security (T4), logical access control (T5), and incident response processes (T8).

T4.name: Communications Security
T4.description: Covers network and communications security: network segregation, routing control, information transfer procedures, secure use of email and messaging, protection of information in transit, and security of network services.
T4.not_includes: User authentication and authorisation rules (T5), operational logging (T3), and supplier/third-party service management (T6).

T5.name: Access Control
T5.description: Covers identification and authentication and logical access control: access control policy, user registration and deregistration, privilege management, password and credential policies, network and system access, application access restriction, and mobile/remote access.
T5.not_includes: Physical access to sites and rooms (T2), HR joiners/movers/leavers processes (M4), and network design aspects (T4).

T6.name: Third Party Security
T6.description: Covers security requirements for third-party service providers, including outsourcing, cloud services, and other suppliers: security clauses in contracts, service level monitoring, changes to services, and on-going assurance.
T6.not_includes: Internal HR employment terms (M4), development supply chain specifics (T7), and general asset management (T1).

T7.name: Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance
T7.description: Covers secure acquisition and development of information systems: security requirements, secure coding practices, testing, change control for applications, cryptographic controls and key management, vulnerability management, and supply chain security in development.
T7.not_includes: Day-to-day operational change in infrastructure (T3), network communications security (T4), and third-party service delivery management (T6).

T8.name: Information Security Incident Management
T8.description: Covers management of information security incidents and weaknesses: reporting, triage, response procedures, communication plans, evidence collection, post-incident review, and lessons learned.
T8.not_includes: Business continuity and disaster recovery planning (T9), and routine operational monitoring (T3).

T9.name: Information Systems Continuity Management
T9.description: Covers continuity and recovery of information systems: business impact analysis, continuity strategy, IT service continuity planning, disaster recovery procedures, and testing and maintenance of continuity plans.
T9.not_includes: Real-time incident handling (T8) and routine backup operations (T3), although these may feed into continuity capabilities.
